info.name: PROJECT THEMIS - Backend API v7
info.description:
Complete API test collection for PROJECT THEMIS Backend v7

Base URL: http://localhost:8000
WebSocket URL: ws://localhost:8000/ws
API Key: themis-unity-key-2026

All endpoints return JSON with 'success' field and 'data' field.
All keys use camelCase.

Camera naming: car{XX}_cam{YY} (20 cameras, 2 per car)
  car01_cam01 = Car 1, front camera
  car01_cam02 = Car 1, rear camera
  ...
  car10_cam02 = Car 10, rear camera

Import this collection into Postman to test all endpoints.

exec: pm.test('Status is 200', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
pm.test('Has name field', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d).to.have.property('name'); });
pm.test('Name is PROJECT THEMIS', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.name).to.equal('PROJECT THEMIS'); });
pm.test('Has version field', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d).to.have.property('version'); });
pm.test('Status is running', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.status).to.equal('running'); });

request.description:
Get occupancy for all cars in current train.

Response:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "trainId": "SF10-001",
    "station": "Manggarai",
    "timestamp": "2026-07-15T...",
    "cars": [
      {
        "carId": 1,
        "occupancyPct": 45.0,
        "status": "NORMAL",
        "passengers": 90,
        "capacity": 200,
        "cameraStatus": "active",
        "cameraId": "car01_cam01",
        "riskScore": 0.3
      },
      {
        "carId": 2,
        "occupancyPct": 22.5,
        "status": "LOW",
        "passengers": 45,
        "capacity": 200,
        "cameraStatus": "active",
        "cameraId": "car02_cam01",
        "riskScore": 0.1
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

request.description:
Get current AI recommendation from Decision Engine.

Returns recommendation only when there's significant occupancy imbalance (>30% difference).

Response (when recommendation exists):
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "action": "MOVE_PASSENGERS",
    "fromCarId": 4,
    "toCarId": 1,
    "confidence": 0.85,
    "reason": "Car 4 is 92% full. Car 1 is only 45% full."
  }
}
```

Response (no recommendation):
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": null
}
```

request.description:
Load 'empty' scenario - empty train.

Resets state and loads empty train configuration.
Broadcasts occupancy updates for all cars via WebSocket.

Response:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "scenario": "empty",
    "description": "Empty train scenario",
    "carsLoaded": 10,
    "message": "Scenario 'empty' loaded",
    "timestamp": "2026-07-15T..."
  }
}
```

header.description: Required API key for frame uploads

request.description:
Upload camera frame from Car 1 front camera.

Required Header: X-API-Key: themis-unity-key-2026
Params: camera_id=car01_cam01, train_id=SF10-001
Body: multipart/form-data with 'file' field (JPEG/PNG image)

Full AI Pipeline (YOLO ONLY detection):
1. Receive JPEG frame
2. Run YOLO11s detection (person class, conf>=0.5)
3. Calculate occupancy from YOLO results
4. Fuse with existing data (FusionEngine)
5. Predict trend (CALESEngine)
6. Generate warning (DecisionEngine)
7. Update State Manager
8. Broadcast via WebSocket

Response:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "frame_id": "frame_1721056800.123",
    "camera_id": "car01_cam01",
    "station_id": "unknown",
    "train_id": "SF10-001",
    "timestamp": "2026-07-15T...",
    "status": "processed",
    "persons_detected": 5,
    "detection_source": "yolo",
    "occupancy": {
      "person_count": 5,
      "capacity": 200,
      "occupancy_percentage": 2.5,
      "status": "LOW",
      "risk_score": 0.1,
      "car_id": 1
    },
    "prediction": {
      "predicted_occupancy": 3.1,
      "risk_score": 0.1,
      "confidence": 0.7,
      "horizon_minutes": 15
    },
    "recommendation": null,
    "warning": null
  }
}
```

exec: pm.test('Status is 200', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
pm.test('Has success field', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d).to.have.property('success'); pm.expect(d.success).to.be.true; });
pm.test('Has frame data', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.data).to.have.property('persons_detected'); });
pm.test('Camera ID is car03_cam01', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.data.camera_id).to.equal('car03_cam01'); });
pm.test('Detection source is yolo', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.data.detection_source).to.equal('yolo'); });

```json
}
```

item.name: 10 - Quick Test Flow
item.description: Run these in order to test the full system flow

exec: pm.test('Backend is healthy', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

request.description: Step 1: Verify backend is running.

exec: pm.test('Scenario loaded', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.success).to.be.true; });

request.description: Step 2: Load peak hour scenario to generate data.

exec: pm.test('Has car data', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.data.cars.length).to.be.greaterThan(0); });

request.description: Step 3: Verify occupancy data loaded from scenario.

exec: pm.test('Has recommendation data', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d).to.have.property('data'); });

request.description: Step 4: Check if AI generated a recommendation.

exec: pm.test('Has warnings data', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.data).to.have.property('warnings'); });

request.description: Step 5: Check if any warnings were generated.

exec: pm.test('Has system state', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.data).to.have.property('train'); });

request.description: Step 6: Verify full system state.

exec: pm.test('Has 20 cameras', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.data.mappings.length).to.equal(20); });

request.description: Step 7: Verify 20 camera mappings.

exec: pm.test('Reset successful', () => { const d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.success).to.be.true; });

request.description: Step 8: Reset simulation for next test.
